note: The Canon translation corpus (R-068) — Secondary Canon texts rendered into Amraqiyyah per the Canon's own mandate ('Translated into Arabiyah al-Amraqiyyah'; docs/03-CANON.md). Source language is authoritative in ambiguity (Canon hierarchy rule 5), so the source line travels with every verse. Fields: src = source text; amr/display per phrases.json conventions; en = English rendering of the Amraqiyyah; note = translator's note.

lines.amr: Al·hina nin·ta i·qadira jatato, la pe al·hina dahīr.
lines.display: Al·hina nin·ta i·qadira jata·to, la pe al·hina dahīr.
lines.en: The Way that one can speak is not THE substrate-eternal Way.
lines.note: al· — the sacred THE for the Way itself (Ali's correction: this Way is never merely worldly); dahīr — 常 as the substrate-eternity (R-069): the Tao that contains time, as Laozi's ch.25 'standing alone, unchanging' demands; ḏet re-ruled to geometric infinity and withdrawn here. R-072: 可 restored — i·qadira + bare-stem complement (the ability auxiliary, AAVE 'can speak'), resumptive on the content verb; and the Mawangdui scrolls vindicate dahīr — the original text read 恆 heng 'enduring' (道可道也非恆道也), changed to 常 only by Emperor Wen's naming taboo: Laozi's own word was closer to dahr than the received text let on

lines.amr: Al·rini nin·ta i·qadira ranato, la pe al·rini dahīr.
lines.display: Al·rini nin·ta i·qadira rana·to, la pe al·rini dahīr.
lines.en: The name that one can name is not THE substrate-eternal name.
lines.note: al·rini in parallel — the eternal Name wears the sacred article as the Way does; R-072: 可 restored in parallel with line 1 (i·qadira + bare stem)

lines.amr: Al·bilā-rini pe na·tap na·pet ma na·yaki.
lines.display: Al·bilā-rini pe na·tap na·pet ma na·yak·i.
lines.en: THE Nameless is the origin of heaven and earth.
lines.note: bilā-compound (R-068): bilā-rini 'the Without-Name'; tap = head→origin, the Egyptian semantics of tp restored. ERRATUM R-071 (Ali's catch): the article was missing — the Nameless is an absolute, and absolutes wear the sacred THE (b is a moon letter: al· unassimilated)

lines.en: THE Named is the mother of the myriad things.
lines.note: het-rini 'the With-Name'; 萬物 as na·pa·šeyāt, the myriad things; iška — the mother-word, inalienable everywhere else, standing bare here as pure role (R-033's citation use). ERRATUM R-071: al· added in parallel with line 3 — the Named is the same absolute seen from the manifest side (h is a moon letter: al· unassimilated)

lines.amr: Ra·pen: en dahr bilā šiweqī, i·hariwa na·biṭenya.
lines.display: Ra·pen: en dahr bilā šiweqī, i·hariwa na·biṭenya.
lines.en: Therefore: ever without desire, one beholds the hidden.
lines.note: šiweqi — the diaspora longing serving for 欲; biṭeni = the batin (~Al-Bāṭin, Threshold Name, Hex 26). ERRATA R-071: bilā governs the genitive (šiweqī, i+i→ī — as line 9's en·ribesī always knew), and the dropped 常 returns as en dahr 'in the aeon, ever' (R-069's own root)

lines.amr: En dahr het šiweqī, i·hariwa na·ẓiherya.
lines.display: En dahr het šiweqī, i·hariwa na·ẓiherya.
lines.en: Ever with desire, one beholds the manifest.
lines.note: ẓiheri = the zahir (~Az-Zāhir, Hex 33) — Laozi's hidden-and-manifest IS the Way's own Threshold pair. ERRATA R-071: het governs the genitive (šiweqī); 常 restored as en dahr, parallel to line 5

lines.amr: Na·sinu nai — en·sinu i·ḫarijasen, we·pen riniātsen ne sinu.
lines.display: Na·sinu nai — en·sinu i·ḫarija·sen, we·pen rini·āt·sen ne sinu.
lines.en: These two — together-as-pair they emerge, but their names are two.
lines.note: en·sinu 'in-two, as a pair' — the together-adverbs (R-069): en·wa as-one, en·sinu as-a-pair; 同出 restored to a true 'together' instead of the earlier dissolve. ERRATUM R-071: riniāt·to (3SG 'its names') corrected to riniāt·sen — the two own their names in the plural, as the verb i·ḫarija·sen already conjugated. R-073: ne added (the plural identificational copula's first attestation — the copula doctrine: pe/ne obligatory for nominal equations in the sacred register); Na·sinu nai stands RATIFIED as the solemn postposed demonstrative — Middle Egyptian zj pn syntax surfacing in the register that reaches for antiquity (preposed pen remains the default everywhere)

lines.en: Named as one: THE primordial Dark.
lines.note: en·wa 'in-one, as one' for 同謂 — named-as-one; ranwia, the passive of the ren-root, arrives free from the engine (R-025 -wi-)

lines.en: Dark within primordial dark — the gate of all mysteries.
lines.note: seba — star, gate, and teaching in one root (S-B-ʾ): the Tao closes on the door the education ladder climbs; biṭeniāt pluralizes by R-060's own law. R-073: pe added — dark-within-dark IS the gate: a nominal equation, and the copula doctrine makes the equals sign obligatory in the sacred register

then as Ali steers through the Secondary Canon
